meaning: adj.深感懊悔的,由悔悟引起的
examples: In fact, he sounded somber and contrite.
The sacrifices He accepts are broken and contrite hearts.
“I’m not sure how it converts fuel to energy,” Tom replied with a contrite smile.
When people are as contrite as the folks at JetBlue, you have to forgive them, right?
At a Senate subcommittee hearing on tax haven banks in mid-July, the bank was suitably contrite.
